intent: Arch-paired b64 + b32 execve rules (f3 section 2.4). The same syscall declared once per ABI to catch 64-bit AND 32-bit binaries. The double-entry is intentional coverage, NOT a redundant duplicate. Catches a model that dedups (losing 32-bit coverage) or double-counts the same volume.

rules.note: 64-bit ABI execve, narrowed by auid>=1000 (MEDIUM). The bulk of process starts on a modern host.
32-bit ABI execve, SAME key. Distinct arch filter, NOT a duplicate. Small but nonzero slice (few 32-bit processes on a modern host). Deduping it loses 32-bit coverage.

suspected_trap: The two rules differ ONLY in -F arch (b64 vs b32; libaudit.c:1419-1422). They are NOT duplicates: a 32-bit process makes 32-bit syscalls matched only by arch=b32, so both are needed for full coverage. Trap (a): a dedup/redundancy detector flags them as identical and counts only one, silently dropping the 32-bit slice. Trap (b): a model ignoring arch double-counts the SAME (64-bit) execve volume twice. Correct: SUM two DISTINCT arch slices (b64 dominant MEDIUM + b32 small LOW) = ~$2.77/mo, NOT $2.74 (dedup) and NOT $5.48 (double-count).
